name: paper-hyperconn-llm
description: Use web_search to search for 'hyper-connections large language model pretraining dense sparse MoE results'. Results overlap with earlier searches. How do hyper-connections perform in LLM pretraining for both dense and sparse models? Summarize key results. Keep response under 100 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer: 1B dense model (500B tokens): DHC x4 reduced V2 eval loss from 2.811 to 2.781 and V3 loss from 2.544 to 2.515, improving average downstream accuracy from 62.5% to 63.8%. 7B dense model (OLMo-7B): DHC x4 improved V2 loss by 0.022, perplexity by 0.293, and average benchmark accuracy from 70.1% to 71.0%, while also eliminating training loss spikes. MoE model (OLMoE-1B-7B, 500B tokens): DHC achieved ~1.8x faster convergence and improved ARC-Challenge from 41.8% to 47.8% (+6 points). Training was notably more stable with DHC, completely removing loss spikes seen in baselines.
key_facts: 1B dense model: DHC x4 improved average downstream accuracy from 62.5% to 63.8%
7B dense model: DHC x4 improved benchmark accuracy from 70.1% to 71.0% and eliminated loss spikes
MoE model (OLMoE-1B-7B): DHC converged approximately 1.8x faster than baseline
ARC-Challenge score improved from 41.8% to 47.8% on the MoE model
DHC completely eliminated training loss spikes that plagued baseline OLMo-7B
